mnemonics: HARM: Heavy metals (like Lead, Arsenic, Cadmium) Are Released from e-waste into the soil and water, causing harm.
keyPoints: E-waste contains hazardous materials like lead, mercury, and cadmium that can leach into soil and groundwater.
Improper disposal of e-waste in landfills leads to the release of toxic substances into the natural environment.
The informal recycling of e-waste, often in developing countries, causes severe air, water, and soil pollution through crude methods like open burning.
commonMistakes: Believing that burying e-waste in a landfill safely contains its toxins, when in fact they often leach into the environment.
Assuming all e-waste recycling is environmentally friendly, without considering that informal, unregulated recycling can be highly polluting.
